justification: All three ACs present with exact values ($47.80->47, $0.99->0, 12-month expiry). Both domain rules present and precise. Both architecture functions (earnPoints, checkExpiry) referenced with full signatures and implementation notes including Math.floor. The 365-day vs 12-month terminology discrepancy is explicitly flagged in terminology_alignment with a note about leap-year edge cases in open_questions. No invented information — all content traces back to the described Bird/MJ outputs.

observations.text: AC2 ($0.99 -> 0) explicitly preserved with explanation that floor of 0.99 is 0, not 1
Terminology alignment section clearly flags the 365-day vs 12-month equivalence assumption and notes leap-year risk
Architecture guidance includes loyalty.ts file location, Math.floor usage, and full function signatures with types
Domain rules include testable assertions that directly encode the exact AC values
Open questions section raises the leap-year concern without inventing a resolution, appropriately deferring to Bird
No fabricated features — explicitly notes persistence is out of scope rather than inventing redemption or storage logic

The open_questions section adds considerations (negative purchaseAmount, batch expiry) not mentioned in the source outputs, though these are framed as questions rather than assertions so they don't constitute invented domain rules
